decisions.title: Main goal for V1 is a working MVP.
decisions.detail: The primary objective for the first version (V1) of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a Minimum Viable Product that is functional.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP

decisions.title: V1 MVP deadline: September 1st.
decisions.detail: The working MVP for V1 must be completed by September 1st.

evidence.quote: a working MVP by September 1st

decisions.title: Hard deadline for V1 MVP due to marketing campaign.
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline for the V1 MVP is a hard constraint, driven by the impending marketing campaign launch.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing campaign launch date: September 5th.
decisions.detail: A marketing campaign is scheduled to launch on September 5th, which dictates the V1 MVP deadline.

evidence.quote: the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: V1 must include user authentication.
decisions.detail: The first version of the product must provide user authentication functionality.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication

decisions.title: V1 must include a working project dashboard.
decisions.detail: The first version of the product must include a functional project dashboard.

evidence.quote: a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: System performance requirement: "super fast".
decisions.detail: The system is required to operate with high performance, described as "super fast".

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend technology: React.
decisions.detail: React has been decided upon for the frontend development.

evidence.quote: we are using React for the frontend

decisions.title: Backend technology: Python (FastAPI).
decisions.detail: Python with FastAPI framework has been decided upon for the backend service development.

evidence.quote: Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Python chosen due to existing team libraries.
decisions.detail: The choice of Python for the backend is justified by the team's existing libraries in Python.

evidence.quote: I think it makes the most sense for our team since we already have libraries built in Python.

decisions.title: Database technology: PostgreSQL.
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL will be used as the database.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL.

decisions.title: Database model: Standard relational.
decisions.detail: The database will follow a standard relational model.

evidence.quote: Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Session management: Standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1.
decisions.detail: For V1, standard PostgreSQL sessions will be utilized for session management.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now

decisions.title: Defer Redis for session management to V2.
decisions.detail: The use of Redis for session management will be postponed until V2 of the project.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2

decisions.title: Use standard PostgreSQL sessions to save budget.
decisions.detail: The decision to use standard PostgreSQL sessions for now is driven by a need to save budget.

evidence.quote: to save budget.

decisions.title: Redis may be considered for V2 if performance is a blocker.
decisions.detail: Redis is deferred to V2, but will be re-evaluated if performance issues arise in V1.

evidence.quote: unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: User authentication must not be built in-house.
decisions.detail: The user authentication system should not be developed internally.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: Integrate Firebase Authentication for V1.
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be integrated for the first version of the product.

evidence.quote: for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth chosen for quick integration, social logins, and pricing.
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication was chosen because it is quick to implement, easily handles social logins, and aligns with pricing requirements.

evidence.quote: It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth to support Google sign-in.
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow users to sign in using their Google accounts.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in

decisions.title: Firebase Auth to support email logins.
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow users to sign in using their email addresses.

evidence.quote: and email logins.

decisions.title: User registration to collect email.
decisions.detail: During user registration, the system must collect the user's email address.

evidence.quote: Just email

decisions.title: User registration to collect full name.
decisions.detail: During user registration, the system must collect the user's full name.

evidence.quote: full name

decisions.title: User registration to collect role.
decisions.detail: During user registration, the system must collect the user's role.

evidence.quote: and role.

decisions.title: User registration must collect company name.
decisions.detail: During user registration, the system must collect the user's company name.

evidence.quote: we need to collect company name too!

decisions.title: Company name is required during signup.
decisions.detail: The company name field is mandatory during the user signup process.

evidence.quote: Company name is required during signup.

decisions.title: Address not required for user registration (for now).
decisions.detail: The user registration process does not currently require the collection of the user's address.

evidence.quote: We don't need address

decisions.title: Telephone number not required for user registration (for now).
decisions.detail: The user registration process does not currently require the collection of the user's telephone number.

evidence.quote: or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: Project targets B2B market.
decisions.detail: The Phoenix Project is designed for a Business-to-Business (B2B) market.

evidence.quote: We are B2B.

decisions.detail: The React frontend application will be deployed using Vercel.

Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Backend deployment: Cloud Run for FastAPI app.
decisions.detail: The FastAPI backend application will be deployed using Cloud Run.

evidence.quote: Cloud Run for the FastAPI app

decisions.title: Database in a private VPC.
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL database must be hosted within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

evidence.quote: connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to public internet.
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL database instance is strictly required to be inaccessible from the public internet for security reasons.

evidence.quote: the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.

decisions.title: Non-exposure of DB to public internet is a strict security constraint.
decisions.detail: The requirement for the PostgreSQL instance not to be exposed to the public internet is a strict technical constraint for security.

evidence.quote: This is a strict technical constraint for security.

decisions.title: User authentication flow needs thorough testing.
decisions.detail: The user authentication flow requires comprehensive testing to ensure its correctness and robustness.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.detail: The session token expiration period has been set to 24 hours.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

decisions.title: Token expiration can be adjusted later.
decisions.detail: The 24-hour token expiration is an initial setting and can be modified in the future.

evidence.quote: We can adjust it later.

decisions.title: Dashboard to display active user metrics.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics

decisions.detail: The project dashboard must include and display the current system status.

evidence.quote: system status

decisions.title: Dashboard to display project lists.
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must include and display lists of projects.

evidence.quote: and project lists.

decisions.detail: The specific features and functionalities of the dashboard will be elaborated upon in the upcoming synchronization meeting.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.detail: The responsibility for provisioning Firebase resources and configuration is currently unresolved.

evidence.quote: who is handling the Firebase provisioning?

decisions.title: Firebase config keys needed by next week.
decisions.detail: The configuration keys for Firebase are required by the end of next week.

evidence.quote: I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Mike assigned to Firebase provisioning.
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase provisioning by Mike by July 8th.
decisions.detail: Mike is expected to have the Firebase provisioning completed by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Sync with marketing team on dashboard design next Wednesday.
decisions.detail: A synchronization meeting with the marketing team to discuss the dashboard design is scheduled for next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Develop and integrate user authentication system via Firebase Authentication (Google Sign-In, Email/Password logins).
Implement user registration flow to collect Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name (Company Name is mandatory).
Design and implement PostgreSQL database schema for user profiles, project data, and dashboard metrics.
Develop backend services using Python FastAPI for user management, session handling, and dashboard data aggregation.
Develop frontend application using React for user authentication UI, registration forms, and project dashboard UI.
Implement project dashboard features to display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Implement session management using standard PostgreSQL sessions.
Configure deployment pipelines for React frontend to Vercel and FastAPI backend to Cloud Run.
Configure network connectivity between Cloud Run and PostgreSQL database using Serverless VPC Access within a private VPC.
Perform thorough testing of the user authentication flow.

constraints.text: V1 MVP must be completed by September 1st (hard deadline due to marketing campaign).
User authentication must use a third-party service; no in-house build.
PostgreSQL database must be hosted within a private VPC and not exposed to the public internet for security reasons.
Firebase config keys are required by the end of next week (dependency on Firebase provisioning by Mike by July 8th).

architectural_decisions.text: Database technology: PostgreSQL with a standard relational model.
Session management for V1: Standard PostgreSQL sessions (Redis deferred to V2 for budget reasons).
User authentication solution: Firebase Authentication (chosen for quick integration, social logins, and pricing).
Cloud Run connectivity to PostgreSQL via Serverless VPC Access.

acceptance_criteria: Given the system is launched, When a user attempts to access the application, Then an authentication mechanism is presented.
Given a user successfully authenticates, When they navigate to the primary application page, Then a functional project dashboard is displayed.
Given Firebase Authentication is integrated, When a a user attempts to sign in via Google, Then the system successfully authenticates the user using their Google account.
Given Firebase Authentication is integrated, When a user attempts to sign in via email, Then the system successfully authenticates the user using their email credentials.
Given a new user is registering, When they provide their email address, Then the system collects and stores their email address.
Given a new user is registering, When they provide their full name, Then the system collects and stores their full name.
Given a new user is registering, When they provide their role, Then the system collects and stores their role.
Given a new user is registering, When they provide their company name, Then the system collects and stores their company name.
Given a new user is registering, When the 'Company Name' field is left blank, Then the system prevents registration and displays a validation error indicating it is a required field.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When viewing the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays current active user metrics.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When viewing the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays the current system status.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When viewing the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays a list of projects.
Given a user has an active session, When 24 hours have passed since the session token was issued, Then the user's session token is expired, requiring re-authentication upon next action.
Given the PostgreSQL database is deployed, When an attempt is made to connect from a public internet IP address, Then all connection attempts are blocked by network security configurations.
test_scenarios: Verify successful user registration via email with valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name. Confirm user can log in with these credentials.
Verify successful user login using valid email and password credentials. Confirm redirection to the dashboard.
Verify successful user login using Google Sign-in. Confirm redirection to the dashboard.
Verify that attempting to register with an existing email address displays an appropriate error message.
Verify that attempting to log in with incorrect email/password displays an appropriate error message.
Verify that the dashboard loads correctly and displays placeholder or default data for 'Active User Metrics', 'System Status', and 'Project Lists'.
Verify that after a user logs in, their session remains active for at least 23 hours and 59 minutes.
Verify that after a user logs in, and remains idle for 24 hours and 1 minute, they are prompted to re-authenticate when attempting an action.
edge_cases: Attempt user registration with invalid email formats (e.g., missing @, missing domain, special characters).
Attempt user registration with extremely long inputs for Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields (e.g., character limits).
Attempt user login with an account that has been disabled or locked (if such functionality exists within Firebase).
Test concurrent logins for the same user account from different devices or browsers.
Repeated rapid failed login attempts (e.g., brute-force scenarios) and observe Firebase's rate limiting/security measures.
Accessing protected application routes directly via URL without an active session or with an expired session.
Attempting to establish a direct network connection to the PostgreSQL database from outside the private VPC.
Monitor system responsiveness during typical user operations (login, dashboard load) under simulated moderate load to identify potential performance bottlenecks against the 'super fast' requirement.
unresolved_behaviors: The specific features, data points, and layout for 'Active User Metrics', 'System Status', and 'Project Lists' on the project dashboard are not yet detailed (D042). This prevents creation of specific test cases for dashboard content and functionality.
The quantitative definition of 'super fast' system performance is needed to create measurable acceptance criteria and performance test targets (D007).
Specific validation rules (e.g., minimum/maximum length, allowed characters) for 'Full Name' and 'Role' fields during user registration are not defined.
The expected behavior for the 'Role' field during registration is unclear; is it a free-text field or a selection from a predefined list?

decision_history: The primary objective for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP) (D001). This MVP has a hard deadline of September 1st, driven by a marketing campaign launching on September 5th (D002, D003, D004).
V1 must include user authentication (D005) and a working project dashboard (D006). The system is required to operate with high performance, described as 'super fast' (D007).

The chosen technologies are React for the frontend (D008) and Python (FastAPI) for the backend (D009), with Python chosen due to existing team libraries (D010). PostgreSQL will be used as the database, following a standard relational model (D011, D012).

For session management in V1, standard PostgreSQL sessions will be utilized to save budget (D013, D015). The use of Redis for session management is deferred to V2, unless performance becomes a blocker (D014, D016). Token expiration is initially set to 24 hours, with the flexibility to adjust later (D037, D038).

User authentication will not be built in-house (D017). Instead, Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1 (D018), chosen for its quick implementation, easy social login handling, and alignment with pricing requirements (D019). Firebase Auth will be configured to support Google sign-in (D020) and email logins (D021).

During user registration, the system must collect email (D022), full name (D023), role (D024), and company name (D025). Company name is a required field (D026). Address and telephone number are not required for V1 (D027, D028). The project targets a B2B market (D029).

Deployment will use Vercel for the React frontend (D030) and Cloud Run for the FastAPI backend (D031). The PostgreSQL database must be hosted in a private VPC (D032) and connected via Serverless VPC Access (D033), with a strict security constraint that the database instance is not exposed to the public internet (D034, D035).
The user authentication flow requires thorough testing (D036).

The project dashboard must display active user metrics (D039), system status (D040), and project lists (D041). Specific features of the dashboard will be detailed in the next synchronization meeting (D042).
Mike has been assigned the responsibility of handling Firebase provisioning (D045).
dependencies: The project's V1 MVP deadline of September 1st is a hard dependency on the marketing team's campaign launch scheduled for September 5th (D003, D004).
Finalization of dashboard design requires a synchronization meeting with the marketing team, scheduled for 'next Wednesday' (D047).
Development work requiring Firebase configuration keys is dependent on Mike completing Firebase provisioning by July 8th (D044, D046).
risks: Vague Performance Requirement: The 'super fast' system performance requirement (D007) is subjective and lacks measurable criteria, which could lead to scope creep, rework, or misalignment of expectations. (Impact: High, Likelihood: Medium if not quantified soon)

Performance Bottleneck with PostgreSQL Sessions: The decision to defer Redis for session management to V2 (D014) and rely on standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 (D013, D015) carries a risk that V1 might encounter performance issues (D016), potentially requiring a costly and time-consuming re-evaluation and expedited Redis implementation. (Impact: Medium, Likelihood: Medium)

Hard Deadline Constraint: The September 1st MVP deadline is a hard constraint tied to a September 5th marketing campaign (D003, D004). Any unforeseen delays could directly jeopardize the marketing launch and incur significant business impact. (Impact: High, Likelihood: Medium if execution is not tightly managed)

Security Violation (Database Exposure): There is a strict technical constraint that the PostgreSQL database instance must not be exposed to the public internet (D034, D035). Any failure to adhere to this constraint would represent a critical security vulnerability for the project. (Impact: Critical, Likelihood: Low if diligently enforced, High if neglected)
alignment_gaps: Dashboard Feature Specificity: While high-level features for the dashboard (active user metrics, system status, project lists) have been identified, the detailed features and functionalities still need to be elaborated and agreed upon in the upcoming sync with the marketing team (D042, D047).

Performance Definition: The system performance requirement is currently described as 'super fast' (D007). This needs to be quantified with specific, measurable criteria to ensure a common understanding among stakeholders and to allow for proper testing and acceptance.
open_questions: What are the specific, measurable criteria and acceptable thresholds for the 'super fast' system performance requirement (D007)? (Impact: High)
What are the detailed features, acceptance criteria, and priority for the project dashboard to be discussed and confirmed with the marketing team during the next sync (D042, D047)? (Impact: Medium)
milestones: Firebase provisioning by Mike completed by 2024-07-08 (D046).
Firebase config keys available for development by 2024-07-08 (D044, D046).
Sync with marketing team on dashboard design by 2024-07-03 (assuming 'next Wednesday' from D047 refers to July 3rd).

system_requirements: The system SHALL deliver a Minimum Viable Product (V1) that is functional. (D001)
The system SHALL provide user authentication functionality in V1. (D005)
The system SHALL include a functional project dashboard in V1. (D006)
The system SHALL operate with high performance. (D007)
The user authentication system MUST NOT be built in-house. (D017)
The system SHALL support user sign-in via Google accounts using Firebase Authentication. (D020)
The system SHALL support user sign-in via email using Firebase Authentication. (D021)
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's email address. (D022)
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's full name. (D023)
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's role. (D024)
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's company name. (D025)
The company name field SHALL be mandatory during user registration. (D026)
The user registration process MUST NOT require the collection of the user's address for V1. (D027)
The user registration process MUST NOT require the collection of the user's telephone number for V1. (D028)
The system SHALL thoroughly test the user authentication flow. (D036)
The system SHALL set the session token expiration period to 24 hours. (D037)
The project dashboard SHALL display metrics related to active users. (D039)
The project dashboard SHALL display the current system status. (D040)
The project dashboard SHALL display lists of projects. (D041)
data_flows: Frontend (React) to Firebase Authentication: User sign-in/registration (Google, Email) data flow. (inferred from D018, D020, D021)
Frontend (React) to Backend (FastAPI): User authentication tokens, dashboard data requests. (inferred from D008, D009, D005, D006)
Backend (FastAPI) to Firebase Authentication: User token verification and potential profile synchronization. (inferred from D009, D018, D037)
Backend (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL Database: User profile data storage, project data storage, session management data. (inferred from D009, D011, D012, D013, D022-D026)
Cloud Run (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL Database: Secure connection via Serverless VPC Access. (D033)
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication Integration: Frontend must utilize Firebase client SDK for Google and email sign-in/registration. Backend must utilize Firebase Admin SDK or equivalent API for token verification and user management. (inferred from D018, D020, D021)

Backend (FastAPI) User Registration Endpoint: Method POST. Expected Payload: { "email": "string", "fullName": "string", "role": "string", "companyName": "string", "password": "string" (inferred)}. Response: User profile confirmation or authentication token. (D022, D023, D024, D025, D026)

Backend (FastAPI) User Login Endpoint: Method POST. Expected Payload: { "idToken": "string" (Firebase ID Token)}. Response: Application-specific session token with 24-hour expiration. (inferred from D018, D037)

Backend (FastAPI) Dashboard Endpoints: Endpoints for '/dashboard/metrics/active-users', '/dashboard/status', and '/dashboard/projects'. Specific payload and response structures for these endpoints are pending definition. (inferred from D039, D040, D041, D042)

constraints.text: The V1 MVP must be completed by September 1st.
The September 1st deadline for V1 MVP is a hard constraint driven by a marketing campaign launch scheduled for September 5th.
The PostgreSQL database must be hosted within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
The PostgreSQL database instance MUST NOT be exposed to the public internet due to strict security requirements.
Firebase configuration keys are required by end of next week.
Firebase provisioning must be completed by Mike by July 8th.

architectural_decisions.text: React will be used for frontend development.
Python with the FastAPI framework will be used for backend service development.
PostgreSQL will be used as the database with a standard relational model.
Standard PostgreSQL sessions will be utilized for session management in V1 to save budget, with Redis deferred to V2 unless performance becomes a blocker.
Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1 user authentication, chosen for quick integration, social logins, and pricing.
The React frontend application will be deployed using Vercel.
The FastAPI backend application will be deployed using Cloud Run.
Cloud Run will establish connectivity to the PostgreSQL database via Serverless VPC Access.
Session token expiration is initially set to 24 hours but can be adjusted later.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific definitions and sources for 'active user metrics' to be displayed on the project dashboard? (D039, D042)
What specific information or data points constitute 'system status' for display on the project dashboard? (D040, D042)
What are the required data points, filtering capabilities, and display order for 'project lists' on the dashboard? (D041, D042)
Blocked by: Acceptance criteria and detailed design for the dashboard features from the marketing team sync (owned by PM, D047).

stakeholder.business_summary: The Phoenix Project's primary goal is to launch a functional first version (V1) by September 1st. This strict deadline is crucial to align with an upcoming marketing campaign launching on September 5th, ensuring the product is ready for promotion.
key_impact: Rapid Market Entry: The project is on track for a critical September 1st launch, allowing us to capitalize on the planned marketing campaign and reach our target B2B customers quickly.

Strong Core Product: The first version will include essential user login features and a functional dashboard with key business metrics like active users and project lists, ensuring a valuable initial offering.
Controlled Costs & Security: We're leveraging proven, cost-effective solutions for user management and ensuring robust security by keeping our customer data protected within a private network.
User Data for B2B Focus: User sign-up will collect essential business details like company name and role, aligning with our B2B strategy.

issues.description: The milestone 'Sync with marketing team on dashboard design by 2024-07-03' includes an inferred specific date (2024-07-03) from the decision 'D047: Sync with marketing team on dashboard design next Wednesday.' The original extracted decision only states 'next Wednesday' and does not provide a specific date or context to reliably infer the current date or 'next Wednesday's' date. Presenting an inferred date as a confirmed milestone is an overstatement of certainty.
issues.suggestion: Remove the specific date '2024-07-03' or explicitly state that the date is an assumption based on a hypothetical current date. It should reflect 'next Wednesday' as per the source.

review.reviewer_notes: The report is well-structured and comprehensive across all role views. Most claims are accurately traced to source decisions. However, a critical hallucination was identified in the PM view's milestones section, where a specific date was inferred for a 'next Wednesday' meeting without sufficient source information to confirm it. This issue needs to be corrected before the report can be published.
